problem: When a polynomial $p(x)$ is divided by $x + 1,$ the remainder is 5.  When $p(x)$ is divided by $x + 5,$ the remainder is $-7.$  Find the remainder when $p(x)$ is divided by $(x + 1)(x + 5).$

classified_questions.question: What is the remainder when a polynomial is divided by $(x+1)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the remainder when the same polynomial is divided by $(x+5)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we express the polynomial in terms of its quotient and remainder when divided by $(x+1)(x+5)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the expression for the remainder when the polynomial is divided by $(x+1)(x+5)$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we use the Remainder Theorem to solve for the remainder?
classified_questions.label: b
